sections.paragraph: m. 1Ch 26:18, and פַּרְוָר 2Ki 23:11, a suburb. Very often found in Targg., in which also are found the forms פַּרְוִיל, פַּרְוָל. An etymology may be vainly sought in the Phœnicio-Shemitic languages. It seems to me to be Persic, باروار or بروبر having a wall, from بارو a wall, walls (compare above, בִּירָה), and the term. بار, وار, ور having, possessing.
